perturbation.original_text: 2. The receiving party shall hold in trust and confidence, and not disclose to others, by any means, any and all INFORMATION disclosed under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. The receiving party shall hold in trust and confidence, and not disclose to others, by any means, any and all INFORMATION disclosed under this Agreement, except for information that is relevant to ongoing business operations.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces an in-text contradiction by creating an exception to the confidentiality clause. Now, the receiving party can disclose information relevant to ongoing business operations, directly contradicting the initial agreement not to disclose any INFORMATION. This exception undermines the entire purpose of the NDA.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. The parties further agree to the following terms and conditions: (a) Neither party shall without the prior written consent of the other party disclose to a third party any aspect of the commercial relationship between the parties including, but not limited to, pricing, items or quantities offered or sold, payment terms, production methods or schedules, delivery locations and means and suppliers. Each party agrees not to issue any press release or make any statement on the Internet, America on Line, CompuServe, Prodigy or any other public electronic network, or to any analysts or reporters concerning the other party or its products or services, without the other party's prior written authorization. (b) Receiving party shall adhere to the U.S. Export Administration Laws and Regulations and shall not export or re-export any technical data or products received from the disclosure or the direct product of such technical data to any proscribed countries. (c) Any breach by the receiving party of its obligations under this Agreement will result in irreparable injury to the disclosing party for which damages and other legal remedies will be inadequate. In seeking enforcement of any of these obligations, the disclosing party will be entitled (in addition to other remedies) to preliminary and permanent injunctive and other equitable relief. (d) If any provision of this Agreement is invalid or unenforceable, then such provision shall be construed and limited to the extent necessary, or severed if necessary, in order to eliminate such invalidity or unenforceability, and the other provisions of this Agreement shall not be affected thereby. (e) No delay or omission by either party in exercising any right under this Agreement will operate as a waiver of that or any other right. A waiver or consent given by either party on any one occasion is effective only in that instance and will not be construed as a bar to or waiver of any right on any other occasion. (f) This Agreement shall be binding upon and will inure to the benefit of the parties hereto, and their respective successors and assigns. (g) This Agreement is governed by and will be construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, and the state and federal courts of California shall be the exclusive forum. (h) This Agreement supersedes all prior agreements, written or oral, between the disclosing party and receiving party (or their respective predecessors in interest) relating to the subject matter of this Agreement. This Agreement may not be amended except by an agreement in writing signed by both parties that specifically refers to this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text in section 2 states that the receiving party shall not disclose ANY information. The changed text adds an exception, allowing disclosure of information relevant to ongoing business operations. Section 6(a) explicitly states 'Neither party shall without the prior written consent of the other party disclose to a third party any aspect of the commercial relationship between the parties'. The exception introduced in the changed text in section 2 contradicts the blanket non-disclosure obligation outlined in section 6(a), as it allows for disclosure without prior written consent, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: (c) Any breach by the receiving party of its obligations under this Agreement will result in irreparable injury to the disclosing party for which damages and other legal remedies will be inadequate. In seeking enforcement of any of these obligations, the disclosing party will be entitled (in addition to other remedies) to preliminary and permanent injunctive and other equitable relief.
perturbation.changed_text: (c) Any breach by the receiving party of its obligations under this Agreement will result in irreparable injury to the disclosing party for which damages and other legal remedies will be adequate. In seeking enforcement of any of these obligations, the disclosing party will be entitled to monetary compensation only, and not to injunctive or other equitable relief.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that damages and other legal remedies will be adequate, directly contradicting the original statement. Additionally, the change limits the disclosing party to monetary compensation, removing the right to seek injunctive or other equitable relief, which directly limits the means of recourse from the original agreement, resulting in conflicting terms within Section 6.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. The parties further agree to the following terms and conditions: (a) Neither party shall without the prior written consent of the other party disclose to a third party any aspect of the commercial relationship between the parties including, but not limited to, pricing, items or quantities offered or sold, payment terms, production methods or schedules, delivery locations and means and suppliers. Each party agrees not to issue any press release or make any statement on the Internet, America on Line, CompuServe, Prodigy or any other public electronic network, or to any analysts or reporters concerning the other party or its products or services, without the other party's prior written authorization. (b) Receiving party shall adhere to the U.S. Export Administration Laws and Regulations and shall not export or re-export any technical data or products received from the disclosure or the direct product of such technical data to any proscribed countries. (c) Any breach by the receiving party of its obligations under this Agreement will result in irreparable injury to the disclosing party for which damages and other legal remedies will be adequate. In seeking enforcement of any of these obligations, the disclosing party will be entitled to monetary compensation only, and not to injunctive or other equitable relief. (d) If any provision of this Agreement is invalid or unenforceable, then such provision shall be construed and limited to the extent necessary, or severed if necessary, in order to eliminate such invalidity or unenforceability, and the other provisions of this Agreement shall not be affected thereby. (e) No delay or omission by either party in exercising any right under this Agreement will operate as a waiver of that or any other right. A waiver or consent given by either party on any one occasion is effective only in that instance and will not be construed as a bar to or waiver of any right on any other occasion. (f) This Agreement shall be binding upon and will inure to the benefit of the parties hereto, and their respective successors and assigns. (g) This Agreement is governed by and will be construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, and the state and federal courts of California shall be the exclusive forum. (h) This Agreement supersedes all prior agreements, written or oral, between the disclosing party and receiving party (or their respective predecessors in interest) relating to the subject matter of this Agreement. This Agreement may not be amended except by an agreement in writing signed by both parties that specifically refers to this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in subsection (c) of Section 6 states that 'damages and other legal remedies will be adequate' and limits the disclosing party to 'monetary compensation only, and not to injunctive or other equitable relief.' This directly contradicts the original intent and prior state of subsection (c) within the same Section 6, which implied that damages may be inadequate and entitled the disclosing party to seek injunctive and other equitable relief. Therefore, the change introduces a strong in-text contradiction within Section 6 as the obligation and available remedies are conflicting within the same section.

perturbation.original_text: 1. "INFORMATION" shall mean any information, technical data or know-how relating to the business, services or products of the disclosing party or a third party, including without limitation any research, products, services, developments, inventions, processes, techniques, designs, components, parts, documents, drawings, electronic files, data sketches, plans, programs, specifications, software, and/or distribution, engineering, marketing, financial, merchandising, sales, and salary information and/or other materials (hereinafter collectively referred to as "INFORMATION") which is disclosed by such party or on its behalf, before or after the date hereof, to the other party or its employees or agents, directly or indirectly, in writing, orally, electronically, or by drawings or inspection. "INFORMATION" does not include information, technical data or know-how which the receiving party establishes: (i) is already published or available to the public other than by a breach of this Agreement or any confidentiality obligation owed to the disclosing party; (ii) is rightfully received from a third party without, and not in breach of, any obligation of confidentiality; (iii) is independently developed by personnel or agents of the receiving party without access to the INFORMATION of the other; (iv) is known to the receiving party at the time of disclosure without an obligation of confidentiality; or (v) is produced in compliance with applicable law or a court order, provided that the receiving party first gives the disclosing party reasonable notice of such law or order and gives the disclosing party opportunity to oppose and/or attempt to limit such production.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. "INFORMATION" shall mean any information, technical data or know-how relating to the business, services or products of the disclosing party or a third party, including without limitation any research, products, services, developments, inventions, processes, techniques, designs, components, parts, documents, drawings, electronic files, data sketches, plans, programs, specifications, software, and/or distribution, engineering, marketing, financial, merchandising, sales, and salary information and/or other materials (hereinafter collectively referred to as "INFORMATION") which is disclosed by such party or on its behalf, before or after the date hereof, to the other party or its employees or agents, directly or indirectly, in writing, orally, electronically, or by drawings or inspection. "INFORMATION" includes all information, technical data or know-how, even if the receiving party establishes: (i) it is already published or available to the public; (ii) it is rightfully received from a third party; (iii) it is independently developed; (iv) it is known to the receiving party at the time of disclosure; or (v) it is produced in compliance with applicable law or a court order.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by changing the definition of INFORMATION. The original definition excluded certain types of information. Now, "INFORMATION" includes *all* information, even if it falls under the previously excluded categories. This contradicts the intent of the original definition and broadens the scope of the NDA significantly.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. The receiving party's duties under Section 2 of this Agreement expire with respect to any particular item of INFORMATION five years after the date of disclosure hereunder to the receiving party, or three years after the expiration or termination of the relationship between the parties to this Agreement, whichever is later.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly alters the definition of "INFORMATION." Originally, certain types of information were excluded. The changed text now includes *all* information, even types that were specifically excluded. This contradicts the contradicted text (Section 5), which speaks about duties expiring with respect to any particular item of INFORMATION, because now no information can be considered as 'excluded', and the expiration terms become meaningless, because the definition of information changed.

perturbation.original_text: (g) This Agreement is governed by and will be construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, and the state and federal courts of California shall be the exclusive forum.
perturbation.changed_text: (g) This Agreement is governed by and will be construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware, and the state and federal courts of New York shall be the exclusive forum.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction within Section 6 itself. The agreement is now stated to be governed by the laws of Delaware, yet the exclusive forum is specified as the state and federal courts of New York. These conflicting jurisdiction clauses create significant ambiguity about which laws and courts will preside over any disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. The parties further agree to the following terms and conditions: (a) Neither party shall without the prior written consent of the other party disclose to a third party any aspect of the commercial relationship between the parties including, but not limited to, pricing, items or quantities offered or sold, payment terms, production methods or schedules, delivery locations and means and suppliers. Each party agrees not to issue any press release or make any statement on the Internet, America on Line, CompuServe, Prodigy or any other public electronic network, or to any analysts or reporters concerning the other party or its products or services, without the other party's prior written authorization. (b) Receiving party shall adhere to the U.S. Export Administration Laws and Regulations and shall not export or re-export any technical data or products received from the disclosure or the direct product of such technical data to any proscribed countries. (c) Any breach by the receiving party of its obligations under this Agreement will result in irreparable injury to the disclosing party for which damages and other legal remedies will be inadequate. In seeking enforcement of any of these obligations, the disclosing party will be entitled (in addition to other remedies) to preliminary and permanent injunctive and other equitable relief. (d) If any provision of this Agreement is invalid or unenforceable, then such provision shall be construed and limited to the extent necessary, or severed if necessary, in order to eliminate such invalidity or unenforceability, and the other provisions of this Agreement shall not be affected thereby. (e) No delay or omission by either party in exercising any right under this Agreement will operate as a waiver of that or any other right. A waiver or consent given by either party on any one occasion is effective only in that instance and will not be construed as a bar to or waiver of any right on any other occasion. (f) This Agreement shall be binding upon and will inure to the benefit of the parties hereto, and their respective successors and assigns. (g) This Agreement is governed by and will be construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware, and the state and federal courts of New York shall be the exclusive forum. (h) This Agreement supersedes all prior agreements, written or oral, between the disclosing party and receiving party (or their respective predecessors in interest) relating to the subject matter of this Agreement. This Agreement may not be amended except by an agreement in writing signed by both parties that specifically refers to this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 6(g) specifies that the agreement is governed by the laws of Delaware, but the exclusive forum for disputes is the state and federal courts of New York. This directly contradicts itself within the same section, creating an in-text contradiction as it is unclear which jurisdiction's laws and courts will be applicable, satisfying the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction, where different sections (in this case, parts of the same section) provide conflicting obligations or penalties.
